Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Model 1 Nodes Post Screen Decide Interview Onboard Payroll Close Edges Post Screen Screen Decide Screen Interview Decide Onboard Onboard Payroll Payroll Close Structure It is a StrictPartialOrder This means any topological sort of this DAG is a valid execution trace

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Post must happen first Screen must happen after Post Decide must happen after Screen Interview must happen after Screen Onboard must happen after Decide Payroll must happen after Onboard Close must happen after Payroll

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Is Interview before Decide No edge Interview Decide or Decide Interview They are parallel after Screen Is Interview before Onboard No So Interview can occur after Onboard

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Model 2 loop_onboarding means Onboard can be repeated xor_payroll means either Payroll happens or skip happens Edges Post Screen Post Interview Interview Decide Decide loop_onboarding loop_onboarding xor_payroll xor_payroll Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Post splits to Screen and Interview so Screen and Interview are parallel after Post Decide depends on Interview Screen is not a dependency for Decide

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Define the standard Hire to Retire process Post Job Screen Candidates Conduct Interviews Make Hiring Decision Onboard Employee Add to Payroll Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Logical constraints Post must be first Screen usually precedes Interview Interview usually precedes Decide Decide must precede Onboard Onboard must precede Payroll Payroll must precede Close

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Severity of anomalies Interview before Screen bad practice Decide before Interview very bad Screen not blocking Decide bad Payroll optional bad Interview after Onboard very bad

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze anomalies in Model 1 Good Screen blocks Decide and Interview This enforces screening happens before decision and before interview

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Bad Decide and Interview are parallel Decide can happen before Interview Impact You can make a hiring decision before conducting interviews Interview can happen after Decide and after Onboard

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Conclusion Model 1 Decide is not blocked by Interview So Decide can precede Interview This allows hiring without interview

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze anomalies in Model 2 Good Interview blocks Decide You must interview before deciding

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Bad Screen is parallel to Interview Interview can happen before Screen Screen does not block Decide Result You can decide to hire without screening

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Anomaly 3 Payroll is optional xor payroll

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Impact Employee can be onboarded and hired without being added to payroll This is a severe business rule violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Model 1 allows Interview after Close Model 2 allows Screen after Close

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 2 seems worse because it has multiple critical anomalies Decide without Screen and Payroll is optional Model 1 preserves Screen and Payroll

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 2 allows Payroll to be skipped If Payroll is skipped the employee is not paid The process fails its purpose

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 requires Screen before Decide Model 2 does not Model 1 requires Payroll Model 2 does not Model 1 allows Decide before Interview Model 2 requires Interview before Decide

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 seems to enforce more constraints that are likely normative Screening before Decision is normative Screening before Interview is normative Payroll is normative

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Conclusion Model 1 is closer to normative Model 1 preserves critical business rules of Screening and Payroll whereas Model 2 allows skipping critical steps
